\left\{ \begin{array}{l} Ax+By+Bz=M\\ \dots\\ \dots \end{array} \right. \to \left[ \begin{array}{ccc|c} A&B&C&M\\ \dots \\ \dots \end{array}\right] \\ \text{Diagonalizamos el lado izquierdo (modificando con los mismos pasos el derecho) y tendremos la solución de cada variable.}

\boxed{\begin{array}{c} \int dx = x + C \\ \int x\,dx = \frac{x^2}{2} + C \\ \int x^2 dx = \frac{x^3}{3} + C \\ \int x^n dx = \frac{x^{n+1}}{n+1} + C\ (n\neq -1) \\ \int \frac{dx}{x} = \ln|x| + C \\ \int e^x dx = e^x + C \\ \int a^x dx = \frac{a^x}{\ln a} + C\ (a>0,\ a\neq 1) \\ \int \sin x\,dx = -\cos x + C \\ \int \cos x\,dx = \sin x + C \\ \int \tan x\,dx = -\ln|\cos x| + C \\ \int \sec^2 x\,dx = \tan x + C \\ \int (1+\tan^2 x)\,dx = \tan x + C \\ \int \csc^2 x\,dx = -\cot x + C \\ \int \frac{dx}{\sqrt{1-x^2}} = \arcsin x + C \\ \int \frac{dx}{1+x^2} = \arctan x + C \\ \int \frac{dx}{a^2+x^2} = \frac{1}{a}\arctan\left(\frac{x}{a}\right) + C \\ \text{\underline{\textbf{Suma:}} } \int (u\pm v)\,dx = \int u\,dx \pm \int v\,dx \\ \text{\underline{\textbf{Partes:}} } \int u\,dv = uv - \int v\,du \\ \text{\underline{\textbf{Barrow:}} } \int_a^b f(x)\,dx = F(b) - F(a) \end{array}}
